scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Establishment of New Fresh Water Fin Fish Hatchery - Gujarat
page_title: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Establishment of New Fresh Water Fin Fish Hatchery - Gujarat

details: The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)" with the component of the Establishment of New Fresh Water Fin Fish Hatchery, introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims provide financial assistance for various inland fisheries activities, establishment of freshwater hatcheries, and support for advanced fish farming methods are provided to individuals, societies, self-help groups, associations, reservoir leaseholders, and private entities involved in inland fisheries activities. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
Increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities.
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries.

Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained on less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits: For the Establishment of New Fresh Water Fin Fish Hatchery, the unit cost of the project is ₹30,00,000/-. Assistance is provided as follows:
Women, Scheduled Caste (SC), and Scheduled Tribe (ST) categories:
60% contribution from the Government of India.
Once in a lifetime benefit per eligible beneficiary for this specific component.
eligibility: The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.

The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.

Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

documents_required: Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport (as proof of identity).
Land Ownership Documents (if applicant has own private land).
Consent Form of Co-owners (in case of co-owned land).
Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Certificate (for Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries).

faqs.question: What is the primary goal of this scheme for inland fisheries?
faqs.answer: The main objective is to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and to create employment opportunities for people involved in inland fisheries.

faqs.question: How does this scheme aim to accelerate inland fish farming activities?
faqs.answer: It aims to accelerate inland fish farming by boosting activities in freshwater bodies, increasing production, and promoting inland fisheries.

faqs.question: Can fish production be increased in limited land and water resources under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, by creating fish tanks using methods like Biofloc with source and Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS), more fish can be produced in less land and water.

faqs.question: What is the benefit of increasing the number of beneficiaries under the subsidy of this scheme?
faqs.answer: If more beneficiaries receive the subsidy, more fish production can be achieved with less water source and less land.

faqs.question: Can fish tanks be created with less land and less water under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, by creating fish tanks using Biofloc with source or Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS), more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to take advantage of the components of this scheme under inland schemes?
faqs.answer: Individuals, societies, self-help groups, associations, reservoir leaseholders, and private entities involved in inland fisheries activities can take advantage of the components.

faqs.question: What percentage of assistance is provided for the general category under the "Establishment of New Fresh Water Fin Fish Hatchery" component?
faqs.answer: 40% of the unit cost is provided as assistance for the general category.

faqs.question: What percentage of assistance is provided for women beneficiaries under this hatchery component?
faqs.answer: 60% of the unit cost is provided as assistance for women beneficiaries.

faqs.question: What is the minimum time limit for reclaiming benefits for the "Establishment of New Fresh Water Fin Fish Hatchery" component?
faqs.answer: The minimum time limit for reclaiming benefits is once in a lifetime.
